name: US500 M5 BB+RSI Mean Reversion with Volatility Gate
hypothesis: US500 on M5 is structurally mean-reverting during RTH (13:30-20:00 UTC) when not in strong trend (ADX<20). A close outside BB(20, 2.0) with RSI(7)<10 (long) or >90 (short), filtered by BB-width above 30th percentile, reverts to the middle band within 30 bars with positive expectancy. Falsifiable: if ADX<20 subset does not outperform ADX>=20 subset, the regime filter is invalid.

entry_rules.long: close < bb_lower AND rsi(7) < 10 AND regime_active AND session_active AND bars_since_last_trade >= 3
entry_rules.short: close > bb_upper AND rsi(7) > 90 AND regime_active AND session_active AND bars_since_last_trade >= 3

notes: Mean reversion applied to an index (US500) rather than XAU — per generation review, XAU M15 mean-rev failed due to trending regime. ADX<20 filter explicitly excludes trending conditions, directly testing whether regime gating rescues the family.
